scope_note: QA Witt Tower Seismic Phase Orbit Discriminator cert [444]. Empirical cert applying the Witt tower three-tier orbit partition (MOD=27, T0=bins 0-8 Singularity neighborhood, T1=bins 9-17 Satellite neighborhood, T2=bins 18-26 Cosmos neighborhood) to the 2011 Tohoku M9.1 earthquake waveform at IU.ANMO LHZ (IRIS public domain timeseries, 05:45-07:30 UTC). DC-detrended by pre-event quiet mean; 60-second RMS windows (N=85); rank-normalized to Z/27Z; consecutive window pairs (bin[t], bin[t-1]) as QA state. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) 6300 samples, 85 windows, 12 quiet windows PASS. (C2) All 12/12 quiet windows in T0 (Singularity neighborhood, bins 0-8); hypergeometric p=3.94e-7 under random-assignment null PASS. (C3) 14/18 surf_peak windows in T2 (Cosmos neighborhood, bins 18-26); hypergeometric p=1.41e-5 PASS. (C4) Quiet and surf_peak use disjoint tier sets ({T0} vs {T1,T2}); zero overlap PASS. (C5) Mean orbit tier increases monotonically across phases: quiet=0.0, P_coda=0.091, S_coda=0.800, surf_peak=1.778 PASS. (C6) Witt v_3 valuation highest for quiet (mean=0.909 > uniform null 0.481) PASS. ORBIT TRANSITION: quiet=100% T0->T0 pairs, P_coda=91% T0->T0, S_coda=33% T0 (transition zone), surf_peak=78% in T2 (0% in T0). Consistent with structural cert [110] singularity->satellite->cosmos orbit-class prediction for seismic phase progression. Theorem NT: RMS amplitudes are observer projections; rank bins are QA integer state; quiet_mean DC removal is observer detrend and does not re-enter QA dynamics. 6 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: IRIS Incorporated Research Institutions for Seismology IU.ANMO LHZ timeseries public domain endpoint; Wall, D.D. (1960) Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Witt tower period theory). Structural parent: cert [110] QA Seismic Orbit (singularity=quiet, satellite=P-wave, cosmos=surface-wave algebraic prediction). Derived and validated 2026-06-18.
